title: tulipa strangulata subsp. variopicta
intro: Tulipa strangulata subsp. variopicta is a subspecies of the Tulipa strangulata plant, commonly known as the 'variegated tulip', a flowering bulbous perennial native to Central Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits associated with Tulipa strangulata subsp. variopicta include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its bulbs and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions.
